title: Can't use rootless with unprivileged overlayfs
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to run podman rootless with overlayfs as the storage driver since it should be working unprivileged nowadays. However, no matter which podman command I run, I get `Error: configure storage: failed to mount overlay for metacopy check with "" options: permission denied`.

strace shows this:

```
[pid  1276] mount("overlay", "/home/adrian/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/metacopy-check3153690312/merged", "overlay", 0, "lowerdir=/home/adrian/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/metacopy-check3153690312/l1,upperdir=/home/adrian/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/metacopy-check3153690312/l2,workdir=/home/adrian/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/metacopy-check3153690312/work,userxattr" <unfinished ...>
[pid  1276] <... mount resumed>)        = -1 EACCES (Permission denied)
```

My dmesg also shows:

```
[   36.604008] overlayfs: upper fs does not support tmpfile.
```

which I'm not sure if it is related or not. I have a very simple setup with `/` being an ext4 filesystem.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Install Alpine Linux, `apk add podman`, enable cgroupsv2 service, add subuids/subgids and then try to use podman rootless.

### Describe the results you received

Podman doesn't work at all.

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should work?

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version 5.5.2
Linux orion 6.16.0-rc4-next-20250704 #1-postmarketOS SMP PREEMPT Fri Jul  4 16:59:37 UTC 2025 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

This is using postmarketOS, which is essentially just Alpine Linux with systemd.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
can't see anything wrong there.

Can you please run `podman --log-level debug ...` and attach the output?

Also please include the output from `podman info` and what files are under `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/containers/overlay`

> What are the permissions on the storage dir and the parent directories, anything unusual there?

That was a very good hint :) They were owned by `adrian:root`, just like my home dir. If I change the owner to `adrian:adrian`, it works just fine. I assume this was because I had renamed the user, group and home dir with `usermod`/`groupmod` before, which must have messed up the permissions. It works just fine now that that is changed!

small note: even `podman info` errors when this is the case!
